title: Files tab: Stale file detection

title: Agent Hub: Evidence Integration
description:
Add evidence capture to Agent Hub with feature linking.

Features:
1. Evidence button in Agent Hub header
2. Feature selection modal (existing feature OR Quick Feature auto-create)
3. Quick Feature creates FEAT-DEBUG-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS with single criterion
4. Uses existing /api/artifacts/refresh endpoint + capture-evidence.js
5. Evidence link in chat → click opens EvidenceViewerModal
6. Agents access evidence via existing artifact API (URL references)

Files to modify:
- backend/app/api/features.py - Add POST /api/features/quick endpoint
- frontend/components/agents/EvidenceCaptureModal.tsx (new)
- frontend/components/agents/AgentPanel.tsx - Add Evidence button + chat display

Key decision: All evidence linked to features (no orphans). Debug features auto-created for quick captures.

Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/clever-pondering-candle.md

title: Agent Hub: Browser interaction for testing
description:
Give agents in Agent Hub the ability to interact with the website for exploration and testing:
1. Click elements on the page
2. Type into form fields
3. Navigate between pages
4. Expand/collapse sections
5. Execute JavaScript for advanced interactions

Implementation approach:
- Use existing Chrome DevTools MCP (mcp__chrome-devtools__* tools)
- Agent can describe what to click/interact with
- System translates to appropriate DevTools commands
- Results shown in Agent Hub chat (success/failure + new state)

Use cases:
- 'Click the AAPL row to expand it'
- 'Navigate to the trading page'
- 'Fill in the symbol field with MSFT and click Add'
- 'Scroll to the bottom of the watchlist'

Dependencies: Chrome DevTools MCP already configured

title: Files tab: Git history integration

Backend: git log --follow integration for file_scanner or new service

title: Agent Hub: Connect to Chrome DevTools MCP
description:
Wire the Agent Hub (dev-companion backend) to use Chrome DevTools MCP tools:

Current state:
- Chrome DevTools MCP is configured in Claude Code
- Agent Hub uses its own WebSocket connection to dev-companion
- MCP tools are not directly accessible from Agent Hub

Required:
1. Expose Chrome DevTools MCP tools through dev-companion API
2. Allow agents to call screenshot/click/navigate from Agent Hub
3. Forward tool calls and results between Agent Hub and MCP

Architecture options:
A) dev-companion calls MCP server directly
B) dev-companion calls Chrome DevTools Protocol directly (skip MCP)
C) Agent Hub calls MCP via a bridge API

This is the foundation task - must be done before screenshot/interaction beads can work.

title: Agent Hub: Screenshot and console error display
description:
Give agents in Agent Hub the ability to:
1. Take screenshots of the current page (or any page)
2. Capture and display console errors/warnings
3. Show this information in the Agent Hub chat

Implementation approach:
- Use existing Chrome DevTools MCP (mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot, etc.)
- Add a 'Page State' panel in Agent Hub showing:
  - Current URL
  - Console errors (red badges with count)
  - Quick screenshot button
- When agent requests screenshot, display it inline in chat
- Wire agents to automatically use these tools when discussing UI issues

Dependencies: Chrome DevTools MCP already configured

title: Fix lint/type errors in symbols.py and file_scanner.py
description:
Pre-commit hooks failing on recent additions:

ruff errors (symbols.py):
- B905: zip() without explicit strict= parameter (7 occurrences)
- PLC0206: Extracting value from dictionary without .items()

mypy errors (file_scanner.py):
- Multiple 'Value is not indexable' errors
- Multiple '.isoformat' on wrong types
- Import error for removed qa_scanner module

mypy errors (symbols.py):
- DatabaseConnection has no attribute 'description' (7 occurrences)
- Missing type parameters for generic dict

Files:
- backend/app/api/symbols.py
- backend/app/services/file_scanner.py
- backend/app/tasks/qa_tasks.py (remove qa_scanner import)
